actionUnits.condition: Precondition: This object uses script GUID 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d (Fire MonoBehaviour). It has a BoxCollider (IsTrigger=0), no Rigidbody. In-scene overrides set debug=1 and maxNeighborDistance≈3.5 via PrefabInstance modifications; other serialized Fire fields from scene: isOn=false, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, onNeighborsThreshold=2, minNeighborDownDistance=3, downMultiplier=2, totalHitTime=5, maxHitInterval=2. To exercise the automatic turn-on path in Update() and CheckNeighborState(), arrange at least 2 neighboring Fire components within maxNeighborDistance whose states notify this Fire that they are ON (neighbors must call this.NotifyNeighborState(true) so onNeighbors >= onNeighborsThreshold). Once shouldTurnOn becomes true, wait until timeToOn elapses so Fire becomes isOn and UpdateFireState(true) executes (plays ParticleSystem, notifies neighbors, calls FireController.Instance.FireChanged(+1)).

Precondition: Ensure the Fire is currently ON (from previous task or via an external call to SetIsOn(true)). To test the extinguish logic, repeatedly invoke the IWaterInteractable path by calling Fire.WaterHit(Vector3 normal) at intervals less than maxHitInterval (e.g., every 1.0–1.5s) so isTurningOff remains true and currentHitTime accumulates. After totalHitTime (5s) is reached, verify Fire turns OFF, UpdateFireState(true) runs (ParticleSystem stops; neighbors notified; FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1)).

Optional direct state toggle hook (if allowed by test harness): call SetIsOn(true) then SetIsOn(false). This exercises UpdateFireState(false) to verify particle play/stop without neighbor notifications (notify=false path). This helps cover the branch where the state is changed externally without NotifyAllNeighbors.
